definition: Support for incidents, breakdowns, recovery, roadside assistance or related operational disruption during the lease or fleet lifecycle.
maas_relevance: Supports vehicle availability and service continuity, both of which are important for MaaS fleet reliability.
notes: Keep distinct from Insurance / Risk Support unless the evidence only refers to risk cover rather than incident response.

definition: Lease, rental, funding or support capability for commercial vehicles such as trucks, trailers, delivery vehicles, bakkies or logistics fleet assets.
maas_relevance: Extends mobility supply beyond passenger vehicles into logistics, delivery and enterprise transport use cases.
notes: Use Commercial Vehicle Range when the evidence is primarily about breadth of available vehicle types rather than the leasing/support capability.

definition: Availability of a broad set of commercial vehicle types within the provider fleet or product offer.
maas_relevance: A broader vehicle range improves the fit between MaaS-adjacent services and different transport or logistics requirements.
notes: Represents vehicle choice breadth; it does not by itself confirm leasing, finance or lifecycle services.

definition: Ability to define lease duration, mileage, utilisation or repayment terms as part of the customer agreement.
maas_relevance: Term and utilisation parameters help align asset supply with demand patterns, usage intensity and fleet planning.
notes: Keep distinct from Needs-Based Lease Customisation where the evidence refers to broader tailoring of operating conditions or solution design.

definition: Programmes that enable drivers or owner-operators to access vehicles or participate in transport operations through a structured leasing or subscription model.
maas_relevance: Can expand the supply base for mobility or logistics networks by enabling operators rather than only financing fleet owners.
notes: Keep distinct from Driver Management / Behaviour, which concerns managing active driver performance.

definition: Tools or services that monitor, manage or improve driver behaviour, performance or driver-related fleet operations.
maas_relevance: Driver behaviour affects safety, cost, uptime and customer experience in managed mobility fleets.
notes: Evidence may be based on data collection or named driver solutions; classify conservatively where service depth is unclear.

definition: A predictable monthly rental or payment structure for vehicle use, often covering a fixed period, mileage or service bundle.
maas_relevance: Predictable asset costs support MaaS pricing, margin control and budget planning.
notes: Does not automatically imply full maintenance unless maintenance is separately evidenced.

definition: Administrative support for managing fleet records, processes, compliance, service coordination or related back-office fleet activities.
maas_relevance: Administration capability helps scale fleet operations and reduces manual burden in multi-vehicle services.
notes: Keep distinct from Fleet Management when the evidence specifically names administration rather than an end-to-end management proposition.

definition: Collection, analysis or reporting of fleet data such as vehicle condition, fuel, tyres, driver behaviour or operational metrics.
maas_relevance: Fleet data supports optimisation, governance and performance management for MaaS operations.
notes: Keep distinct from Vehicle Tracking / Telematics when the evidence is about reporting outputs rather than live location or telemetry.

definition: Management of fleet operations or the vehicle lifecycle, potentially including procurement, maintenance, administration, risk, reporting and service coordination.
maas_relevance: Fleet management is a core operating capability for placing, maintaining and coordinating vehicle supply within a MaaS ecosystem.
notes: Use more specific capabilities where the evidence identifies a concrete sub-capability such as maintenance, procurement or reporting.

definition: Fuel-related services, optimisation, cost control, data capture or administration for vehicles or fleets.
maas_relevance: Fuel or energy cost control is important for unit economics in usage-based mobility operations.
notes: Public evidence may not always specify whether the service includes cards, billing, controls or reporting.

definition: A lease or rental product that bundles vehicle use with maintenance responsibilities over an agreed term or mileage.
maas_relevance: Bundles asset access with operational readiness, reducing lifecycle complexity for MaaS fleet deployment.
notes: Keep distinct from Maintenance Management, which is the underlying maintenance service capability.

definition: Insurance, risk assistance or related support attached to financed, leased or managed vehicles.
maas_relevance: Risk support helps protect vehicle assets and reduce operational exposure in mobility services.
notes: Keep distinct from Residual Value / Ownership Risk Transfer, which concerns asset ownership and end-of-contract value risk.

definition: A lease structure that gives the customer an option or pathway to buy the vehicle or asset at the end of the agreement.
maas_relevance: Ownership pathways may support driver, operator or partner acquisition models in mobility ecosystems.
notes: Keep distinct from Operating Lease / Rental where no ownership pathway is evidenced.

definition: Support for vehicle licensing, registration, licence renewal or related compliance administration.
maas_relevance: Compliance administration helps keep vehicles legally available for service in managed fleets.
notes: May be bundled with broader fleet administration or lease products.

definition: Maintenance, servicing, roadworthiness, authorisation or maintenance-plan support for leased or managed vehicles.
maas_relevance: Maintenance management protects uptime, safety and service quality for mobility fleets.
notes: Keep distinct from Full Maintenance Lease / Rental, which is a bundled lease product rather than the maintenance capability alone.

definition: Ability to serve multiple customer segments, sectors or fleet contexts such as public and private sector operations.
maas_relevance: Cross-segment coverage may indicate operational adaptability across different MaaS partners, customers or channels.
notes: Represents market/service coverage rather than a specific lease product.

definition: Tailoring lease or fleet solutions to customer needs, vehicle operating conditions, utilisation patterns or use-case requirements.
maas_relevance: Customisation helps align vehicle supply and contract design to specific mobility service models.
notes: Keep distinct from Contract Term / Mileage Structuring where evidence is limited to term or distance settings.

definition: A lease structure positioned around no balloon payments, lower upfront deposits or reduced hidden-cost exposure.
maas_relevance: Lower upfront and back-end payment friction can improve adoption and cash-flow fit for mobility participants.
notes: Specific to commercial payment structure; do not merge with Fixed Monthly Cost / Rental unless only predictability is evidenced.

definition: Digital or online application, account setup or onboarding process for accessing lease products.
maas_relevance: Digital onboarding supports scalable partner, customer or operator activation for MaaS propositions.
notes: Represents origination/access channel rather than the lease product itself.

definition: Vehicle access through lease, rental or subscription-style use for a defined period, usually without outright ownership by the customer.
maas_relevance: Provides the core asset-access mechanism for deploying vehicles into mobility services without traditional ownership.
notes: Keep distinct from Full Maintenance Lease / Rental and Lease-to-Own where those specific product structures are evidenced.

definition: Risk reduction benefit from outsourcing fleet purchasing, management or lifecycle responsibilities to the provider.
maas_relevance: Risk reduction through outsourcing can make fleet participation more viable for MaaS partners and enterprise customers.
notes: Keep distinct from Insurance / Risk Support when the evidence is about operational outsourcing rather than insurance cover.

definition: Access to preconfigured lease offers or packaged vehicle choices that simplify vehicle selection and purchase decisioning.
maas_relevance: Preconfigured offers can speed up vehicle selection and customer activation in digital mobility channels.
notes: Keep distinct from Vehicle Procurement / Selection where the evidence is about sourcing or acquiring vehicles for the customer.

definition: Transfer or management of residual value, ownership, resale or end-of-contract asset-value risk away from the customer.
maas_relevance: Residual-risk handling supports predictable asset economics and fleet renewal planning for MaaS operations.
notes: Keep distinct from Vehicle Disposal / Return, which concerns the operational end-of-contract process.

definition: Ability to support customers from a single leased vehicle through to an entire fleet.
maas_relevance: Scalable vehicle access supports both pilot deployments and larger MaaS fleet rollouts.
notes: Represents scale range, not a standalone lease product.

definition: Tyre replacement, tyre health monitoring or tyre-related services within a lease or fleet management offer.
maas_relevance: Tyre management supports safety, uptime and cost control for high-utilisation mobility fleets.
notes: May be included within full maintenance products, but remains a specific operating capability where separately evidenced.

definition: Support for vehicle return, disposal or end-of-contract handling at the end of a lease or fleet lifecycle.
maas_relevance: End-of-life handling enables disciplined fleet renewal, cost control and service-quality management in MaaS fleets.
notes: Keep distinct from Residual Value / Ownership Risk Transfer where the evidence focuses on financial risk rather than operational return or disposal.

definition: Finance or funding structures that enable customers to acquire, lease or access vehicles for business or fleet use.
maas_relevance: Scalable finance is a strategic foundation for placing vehicles into mobility networks without requiring outright purchase.
notes: Keep distinct from Operating Lease / Rental where the evidence is about the usage product rather than the underlying finance or funding capability.

definition: Customer benefit from provider-negotiated vehicle pricing or volume discounts passed through in the lease or fleet arrangement.
maas_relevance: Procurement scale benefits can improve MaaS fleet economics and total cost of ownership.
notes: Keep distinct from Vehicle Procurement / Selection, which concerns sourcing or selecting vehicles.

definition: Support for selecting, sourcing, purchasing, acquiring or configuring vehicles for a customer fleet or lease requirement.
maas_relevance: Procurement and selection are core supply-side capabilities for building, refreshing and scaling mobility fleets.
notes: Keep distinct from Preconfigured Offers / Vehicle Choice where the evidence is about choosing from packaged offers rather than procurement support.

definition: Replacement of leased or managed vehicles during or at the end of the agreed lifecycle.
maas_relevance: Replacement capability supports fleet availability and lifecycle renewal for mobility services.
notes: Keep distinct from Vehicle Disposal / Return, although both may occur in the same end-of-life workflow.

definition: Tracking, telemetry or fleet-management technology used to monitor vehicle location, use or related operational data.
maas_relevance: Tracking and telematics support visibility, utilisation management and operational control in MaaS fleets.
notes: Keep distinct from Fleet Data / Reporting where the evidence is about analysed data or reports rather than tracking technology.
